text:
Once, there was an old owl living in a whispering forest. Every dawn, this wise old owl watched over his woods. One day, as he was scanning the trees for deer breakfast, he spotted a young bird taking flight.

"It seems you're ready to fly," said the owl, chuckling softly.

The young bird paused mid-flight, looking curiously at the old owl.

"I want to learn wingsight gliding," it confessed.

The owl nodded approvingly.

"Then glide you shall," he said. And so they began. Night after night, the young bird practiced.

Years passed, and the young owl became an expert glider. He could navigate effortlessly through the forest.

One autumn day, they flew together deep into the heart of maple woods. They danced amidst Falling Leaves.

As they floated down, an old woman noticed their antics.

"You create quite the spectacle," she remarked.

"Yes, sometimes too much," the owl confessed.
